mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off sentiment and equity repricing downward. A 0.3 percentage point or greater increase in UNRATE over a 3-month period predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth measured by RSAFS indicates robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations. When RSAFS shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5%, equity valuations expand over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors increase growth forecasts.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite strong retail sales signal, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with stable or declining inflation indicates supply-side productivity gains without cost pressures. When INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month and CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth remains below 0.2%, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over 60 trading days as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met compared to baseline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates sharply, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer income growth. When PAYEMS shows month-over-month growth below 0.1% for two consecutive months, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.1% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite weak payroll growth signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline sharply while real GDP growth remains positive, indicating demand rotation away from housing investment toward other sectors. This reallocation produces neutral to slightly negative equity returns over 5 trading days as construction-related earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% MoM while GDPC1 growth remains > 1% annualized
regime_hint: demand reallocation from housing to services
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while inflation measured by CPIAUCSL shows month-over-month growth below 0.15%, indicating monetary policy has successfully tightened financial conditions without triggering deflation. This regime produces positive equity returns over 60 trading days as terminal rate expectations stabilize and risk premiums compress.
regime_hint: successful disinflation with stable policy rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met compared to baseline, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month while unemployment remains stable below 4.5%, indicating supply-side weakness without labor market stress. This signals potential stagflation risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margin compression is priced in.
regime_hint: supply shock with tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met compared to baseline, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.
